title: S6: the startup bridge — a session that has a work item is TOLD to load its context manifest
action: Per D38 (owner directive: loop closure over coverage). Turn the context consumer from a tool nobody calls into the default startup move. (1) inject.mjs (templates/lib SOURCE OF TRUTH + .bee/bin mirror): in the session preamble, when .bee/state.json's active feature has a matching bee.work-item concept in docs/knowledge/ (bee.id == feature), emit a KNOWLEDGE CONTEXT block naming the exact command `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge context --work <feature> --budget 20000` and one imperative line: run it and read the manifest's files before touching code — it is the curated context for this feature, and it replaces scanning docs/history. When NO work item matches, emit nothing (silence, not a nag) — but when the feature is non-idle and has no work item, emit ONE line offering to author one (docs/specs/okf-profile.md Templates section is the reference). Reuse knowledge.mjs's existing concept collection — never a second parser (D12). Preamble cost is bounded: the block is at most ~4 lines, never the manifest itself. (2) AGENTS.md startup list + skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md (SOURCE — AGENTS.md is rendered from it): add ONE numbered startup step after the status step: if the active feature has a work item concept, run knowledge context and read the manifest before planning or execution. Budget: scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs enforces 20 KiB hard (currently 17680 bytes rendered, 17562 block) — keep the addition under ~250 bytes and prove the suite green. (3) skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md Session Scout section: one line matching the same rule. (4) RED-FIRST: extend the inject test suite — a fixture with state.feature set and a matching work item emits the block naming the command; a fixture with no matching work item emits nothing; the no-work-item-but-active-feature case emits exactly the one offer line; the block never contains manifest entries. (5) Propagation sequence LAST (onboard --apply, render_plugin_skill_trees, release_manifest --write), ONE commit.

truths: node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 (65 suites) — INCLUDING scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs green after the AGENTS.md edit
red evidence: the new inject assertions failed before implementation (verbatim)
live proof quoted in the trace: the real session preamble for this repo (feature okf-foundation, which HAS a work item) now carries the knowledge-context block with the exact runnable command
a fixture with no matching work item produces no knowledge-context block (silence, not a nag)
AGENTS.md and the template block both stay under 20480 bytes — quote the byte counts
prohibitions: the preamble never embeds the manifest itself — only the command (context budget discipline)
no second frontmatter parser (D12) — reuse knowledge.mjs
never edit AGENTS.md without the same edit in templates/AGENTS.block.md (rendered artifact)

trace.outcome: Startup bridge live: the session preamble now TELLS a session with a matching bee.work-item to run the knowledge-context command (pointer only, 3 lines, never the manifest); silence when no feature is active; exactly one author-one offer line when an active feature has no work item. AGENTS.md startup step 4 + bee-hive Session Scout carry the same rule. AGENTS.block.md 17562 -> 17821 B, AGENTS.md 17680 -> 17939 B (hard budget 20480). 65 suites green incl. test_agents_budget.
